lede: A look at November 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Luka Romero and Yoon Park, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Utah monolith
events.desc: The Utah monolith, built sometime in 2016 is discovered by state biologists of the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources.

events.title: Texas A&M University
events.desc: At Texas A&M University, the Aggie Bonfire collapses killing 12 students and injuring 27 others.

events.title: South Africa
events.desc: In South Africa, 21 political parties approve a new constitution, expanding voting rights and ending white minority rule.

events.title: King's Cross fire
events.desc: King's Cross fire: In London, 31 people die in a fire at the city's busiest underground station, King's Cross St Pancras.

events.title: Jonestown
events.desc: In Jonestown, Guyana, Jim Jones leads his Peoples Temple to a mass murder–suicide that claimed 918 lives in all, 909 of them in Jonestown itself, including over 270 children.

events.title: Battle of Berlin (RAF campaign)
events.desc: World War II: In the first action of the Berlin Air Offensive, four hundred and forty Royal Air Force planes bomb Berlin causing only light damage and killing 131. The RAF loses nine aircraft and 53 air crew.

events.title: Warship
events.desc: Two United States warships are sent to Nicaragua after 500 revolutionaries (including two Americans) are executed by order of José Santos Zelaya.

events.title: Christian IX
events.desc: King Christian IX of Denmark signs the November constitution that declares Schleswig to be part of Denmark. This is seen by the German Confederation as a violation of the London Protocol and leads to the German–Danish war of 1864.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: In a naval action, French frigates defeat British East Indiamen in the Bay of Bengal.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: Christopher Columbus first sights the island now known as Puerto Rico.

deaths.text: S. R. D. Vaidyanathan, Indian nadaswaram player and composer
Cab Calloway, American singer-songwriter and bandleader (The Cab Calloway Orchestra)
Jim Jones, American cult leader, founded Peoples Temple

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abhai of Hach (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Dedication of Saints Peter and Paul
Christian feast day: Elizabeth of Hungary (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Mabyn (Roman Catholic Church and Anglicanism)
